_about: PUBLIC, cited reciprocal collateral-sensitivity pairs for Burkholderia multivorans. Unlike the BurkData-derived collateral rows (local-only), every pair here traces to a peer-reviewed publication and is redistributable — so the treatment/cycling beat renders on the public deploy WITH provenance, never as a blank panel. These are literature-REPORTED reciprocal CS relationships (resistance to A coincides with re-sensitization to B, and vice-versa), not values computed in this repo; exact per-lineage support counts are intentionally left null rather than fabricated.

citation.title: Reciprocal antibiotic collateral sensitivity in Burkholderia multivorans
citation.note: B. multivorans evolved to resistance against six drugs (chloramphenicol, ceftazidime, levofloxacin, meropenem, minocycline, trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole). Collateral sensitivity was observed in 170/279 evolved strains; reciprocal CS pairs arose in >=25% of evolved strains. The pairs below are the highest-frequency reciprocal CS pairs the authors highlight as most clinically actionable for two-drug switching.
